title: Lebeckia linearis
intro: Lebeckia linearis, commonly known as the "bitter root" or "Karee", is a plant species native to the southern regions of Africa, characterized by its narrow, linear leaves and small, yellow flowers. The health benefits of Lebeckia linearis include its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments, such as digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Lebeckia linearis is typically prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine to make remedies for colds, coughs, and other respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Lebeckia linearis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as legumes. It belongs to the subfamily Faboideae and the tribe Loteae. Within the Loteae tribe, Lebeckia linearis is a member of the genus Lebeckia. The plant is native to South Africa and is further divided into several subspecies. The classification of Lebeckia linearis is an important aspect of understanding its evolutionary history and relationships with other plants.

Its roots and leaves are used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
